{{Quote|Hey! What's that thing doing in here? Why don't you go below-deck where you belong? This Jedi may be a lousy droid-lover, but the rest of us don't want your kind mixing with people!|Joey Orsel, to 68-RKO|<ref name="marvel24" />}}
'''Joey Orsel''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who, during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], was a passenger on a [[pleasure cruiser]] headed for [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] under the command of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Quasar (Human)|Quasar]]. Also on the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] was [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], who was accompanied by a [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] called [[68-RKO]]. A virulent [[Anti-droid sentiment/Legends|hater of droids]], Orsel accosted 68-RKO and ordered it to leave, in the process insulting Kenobi. The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] responded by tripping Orsel, sending the man flying through a nearby card table. Furious, Orsel drew a [[Dagger/Legends|dagger]], waited until Kenobi turned his back and leapt at his foe, only for Kenobi to quickly activate his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and run Orsel through without ever even pivoting to look at him.<ref name="marvel24" />

==Behind the scenes==
Joey Orsel made his first and only appearance in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity in [[Star Wars (1977) 24|''Star Wars'' (1977) 24]], written by [[Mary Jo Duffy]] and released in [[1979]]. Orsel was illustrated by [[Carmine Infantino]].<ref name="marvel24" />
